5.description: Open-ended topic summary: synthesize main points into one paragraph (ideal_answer), same JSON shape as ideal-answer evaluation.

6.description: Summary with XML-tagged context and strict JSON (reasoning + ideal_answer), aligned with prompts_generic #6 style.

1.description: Yes/no exact answer only. Chain-of-thought reasoning, then a single JSON with exact_answer yes or no.

2.description: Null hypothesis framing. Treats 'yes' as a claim that must be proven by the abstracts; defaults to 'no' if evidence is absent, mixed, or inconclusive. Designed to reduce yes-bias.

3.description: Blind verdict framing. Model first writes a single neutral sentence summarising what the abstracts collectively say, then commits to yes or no. Avoids pro/con list structure that anchors toward yes.

4.description: Claim-linked verdict. Model writes one sentence explicitly stating whether the abstracts support or contradict the specific claim in the question, then answers yes or no. Ensures the summary is grounded in the question rather than a generic abstract summary.

1.description: Factoid exact answer only. Up to 5 candidate named entities ranked by likelihood.

2.description: Rank-1 confidence. Model first identifies the single most directly supported entity from the abstracts and commits to it, then lists up to 4 alternatives. Designed to push the correct answer to rank 1 and improve SAcc/MRR.

3.description: Exhaustive coverage. Model explicitly identifies what type of entity the question asks for, then scans every abstract for matching entities before ranking. Designed to improve LAcc by reducing missed answers.

4.description: Abstract-grounded factoid. Explicitly forbids answering from prior knowledge — every candidate must be traceable to a specific statement in the provided abstracts. Targets confabulation, which is especially common in factoid questions where the model is confident it knows the answer.

5.description: Reformulation-then-retrieve. Model first rewrites the question as a fill-in-the-blank statement to narrow the semantic target, then retrieves candidates using the clean baseline format. Combines the coverage benefit of slot-filling reformulation with straightforward ranked extraction.

6.description: Fill-in-the-blank reformulation. Model first rewrites the question as a fill-in-the-blank statement, then scans each abstract for what fills the blank. Reframes open retrieval as targeted slot-filling, producing more precise extractions.

1.description: List exact answer only. All relevant entities as a nested list, each wrapped in its own array.

2.description: Per-abstract enumeration then merge. Model lists qualifying entities from each abstract individually before deduplicating into a final answer. Prevents early stopping by forcing exhaustive coverage of every abstract.

3.description: Recall-biased inclusion. Model is explicitly instructed to err on the side of inclusion — if unsure whether an entity qualifies, include it. Targets the coverage gap by pushing recall up, justified by the near-symmetric P/R balance in the baseline.

4.description: Entity type anchoring + exhaustive scan. Model first declares the type of entity the question asks for, then enumerates exhaustively within that category. Keeps precision high by filtering out irrelevant entity types while allowing full coverage within the right category.

5.description: Precision-focused verify-then-include. Model collects all candidate entities first, then explicitly verifies each one against the question before including it in the final answer. Targets the precision gap by filtering out tangentially related entities that the baseline includes incorrectly.

6.description: Broad sweep then verify. Combines recall-biased collection (p3) with precision-focused verification (p5). Step 1: collect all candidates including borderline ones — if unsure, include. Step 2: verify each against the question and remove anything only tangentially related. Aims to recover recall lost in p5 while preserving its precision gain.
